items.claim: Paddy Pimblett is a high-level grappler with a strong submission arsenal and the ability to chain attempts together.
items.quote: He's got a good submission arsenal as well. He'll chain his submission attempts together.

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett has developed effective elbows and ground-and-pound later in his career.
items.quote: The elbows and the ground and pound are something that he's kind of shifted to later in his career and he's done really well at using that.

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis is a cleaner and more technical striker than Paddy Pimblett.
items.quote: I think Benois is the better striker as far as, you know, technique goes, right?

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis's best weapon is his body kick.
items.quote: His body kick, I've said it many times, his body kick is his best weapon.

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis is durable despite having been stopped three times in his career.
items.quote: So, I say he's durable even though he's been knocked out before and stopped or TKO'ed like a total of of three times

items.claim: Conor McGregor has not won an MMA fight in approximately five years.
items.quote: His last MMA win was um I mean it was I mean it was in the before time, right? The long long ago before the world shut down.

items.claim: Max Holloway holds most of the significant strike volume records in the UFC.
items.quote: Like he's he holds most of the significant strike volume records in the UFC, especially for like standing strikes

items.claim: Max Holloway is an effective volume striker who fights well out of either stance.
items.quote: There is not a more um effective volume striker in the entire MMA world than Max Holloway. ... Fights out either stance.
